items.claim: Conor McGregor suffered a leg injury eight months ago and is currently using a cane.
items.quote: he broke his leg in a fight like eight months ago and he's hobbling around with a cane.

items.claim: Conor McGregor is 37 years old and will turn 38 shortly after the fight.
items.quote: You're coming back, you're 37 years old. Connor turns 38 like 4 days after this fight.

items.claim: Conor McGregor has not won a fight in six and a half years.
items.quote: he's not won in seven years, six and a half.

items.claim: Conor McGregor tore his meniscus during his first fight against Dustin Poirier, which forced him to wrestle.
items.quote: I understand he actually tore his miniscus in that fight, which is why he chose to wrestle.

items.claim: Max Holloway has only been knocked out once in his entire career.
items.quote: Max has been knocked out once in his entire career

items.claim: Conor McGregor struggles against high-volume, pressure-based fighting styles.
items.quote: Conor doesn't deal good with that kind of pressuring having to fight consistently off his back foot.

items.claim: Max Holloway's cardio is not a problem.
Conor McGregor's knockout power is less effective at higher weight classes compared to 145 pounds.
items.quote: all of his big knockouts, they're at 145. He hits you at 145, it's a game over.
